title: ProcRoute Policy
description: Default-deny policy for process-scoped route authorization. Any connection from any process to an internal prefix is denied unless an explicit allow rule matches.

internal_prefixes.description: CIDR blocks that form the protected internal route set (I). Connections to these prefixes are denied by default for all processes.

applications.description: Per-application allow rules. Each entry grants a matched application access to a subset of internal prefixes.

app_id.description: Unique identifier for this application principal.

match.description: Criteria to bind processes to this application identity.

allow.description: Destination rules this application is permitted to reach.

cgroup.description: Cgroup v2 path pattern. Exact match or glob with a trailing '/*' to match a subtree (e.g. '/system.slice/corp-browser.service' or '/user.slice/user-1000.slice/app-*.scope/*').

exec_hash.description: Optional SHA-256 hex digest of the main executable. When set, the daemon verifies the hash before adding the cgroup-to-app binding. This hardens identity against binary replacement.

prefixes.description: CIDR blocks this rule grants access to. Must be subsets of internal_prefixes.

ports.description: Optional port allowlist. If omitted, all ports on the matched prefixes are allowed. Each entry is a single port (integer) or a 'low-high' range string.

protocol.description: Transport protocol. Defaults to 'any' (both TCP and UDP).
